eliminated_jobs.title: Agile Delivery Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
As an Agile Delivery Manager, you will lead the delivery of new digital services that support and improve access to essential services for the people of Barbados. You will collaborate with a product manager, user researcher, service designer, developers, and government subject matter experts. You will shape the team culture, establish agile ways of working, set up the agile environment, and coach team members in agile practices. You will also act as a servant leader, removing impediments, managing risks and dependencies, and ensuring the team delivers high value within constraints. The role requires experience in establishing new teams, guiding those new to agile, and promoting user-centered design. You will communicate across organizational and political boundaries, translating complex information into accessible language for non-technical audiences and fostering trust and open collaboration.
Essential attributes include deep knowledge of agile tools and techniques, servant leadership, problem-solving, facilitation, delivery focus, strong communication, and visual thinking for mapping user journeys.

eliminated_jobs.title: Product Manager Analytics
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Remote – LATAM

About Us

AccelOne provides custom software development and design services for companies across the U.S. and Latin America. Built on core principles of transparency, communication, and accountability, we deliver high-quality, scalable solutions that support our clients’ long-term success.

About the Project

Our client is a U.S.-based leader in digital experience personalization. Their platform powers real-time, data-driven experiences for top global retail and e-commerce brands. Operating at scale across international markets, they leverage advanced analytics and decisioning engines to optimize customer engagement and performance.

Role Summary

We are seeking a Product Manager focused on Analytics to lead the strategy, roadmap, and execution of analytics and reporting capabilities within a large-scale personalization platform.

In this role, you will work cross-functionally with Engineering, Data Science, UX, and Go-to-Market teams to deliver analytics features that provide clear and actionable insights for marketers. These insights help customers measure campaign effectiveness, understand customer behavior, and optimize digital experiences.

The ideal candidate combines product management expertise with a strong understanding of data, analytics, and marketing performance metrics, and has experience working in SaaS environments.

Responsibilities

Define and manage the product roadmap for analytics and reporting capabilities.

Own and prioritize the product backlog, balancing business value, customer feedback, and technical feasibility.

Write clear user stories, acceptance criteria, and product specifications for engineering teams.

Collaborate closely with Engineering, UX/UI Design, and Data Science teams to deliver high-quality analytics features.

Lead Agile ceremonies, including sprint planning, backlog grooming, and sprint reviews.

Act as the subject matter expert for analytics functionality, communicating product progress and roadmap to stakeholders.

Work with Sales and Customer Success teams to understand customer needs and ensure successful product adoption.

Identify opportunities to enhance dashboards, reporting capabilities, and analytics insights for customers.

Support data-driven decision-making through analytics tools, experimentation, and reporting frameworks.

Required Qualifications

3+ years of experience as a Product Manager, Product Owner, or Business Analyst in a SaaS environment.

Experience defining and launching analytics, reporting, or business intelligence products.

Strong understanding of marketing metrics, sales funnel dynamics, and customer behavior analytics.

Experience working in Agile development environments (Scrum or similar).

Ability to translate complex business requirements into clear product specifications.

Experience collaborating with cross-functional teams including engineering, design, and data teams.

Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication skills and English fluency.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience in Marketing Technology (MarTech) platforms.

Experience working with analytics or BI tools such as Tableau, Looker, Power BI, or similar.

Familiarity with product analytics tools (Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics, etc.).

Understanding of A/B testing, experimentation frameworks, and data-driven optimization.

Experience working with data teams or SQL-based analytics environments.

What We Offer

100% Remote Work across LATAM.

Competitive compensation aligned with experience.

Professional growth opportunities: certifications, training, and workshops.

A collaborative and people-first culture where innovation and well-being come first.

eliminated_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Genomics England is a global leader in enabling genomic medicine and research, focused on creating a world where everyone benefits from genomic healthcare. Building on the 100,000 Genomes Project, we support the NHS’s world-first national whole genome sequencing service and run the growing National Genomic Research Library, alongside delivering numerous major genomics initiatives. By connecting research and clinical care at national scale, we enable immediate healthcare benefits and advances for the future.

Our mission is to provide the evidence and digital systems so that by 2035 genomics could play a role in up to half of all healthcare interactions, whilst securing the UK’s position as the best place to discover, prove and benefit from genomic innovations.

We are accelerating our impact and working with patients, doctors, scientists, government and industry to improve genomic testing, and help researchers access the health data and technology they need to make new medical discoveries and create more effective, targeted medicines for everybody.

Behind the Healthcare and Research outcomes, Genomics England delivers through designing, developing and operating complex healthcare software systems.

We’re on the cusp of big changes with the real prospect of genomics becoming the fabric of everyday healthcare through the lifetime - from birth to old age.

Job Description

We’re looking for a Senior Product Manager to own and evolve a crucial set of data-focused, backend services and platform capabilities, which sit at the core of the Healthcare Genomics Service.

These are critical, production systems used by multiple teams across Genomics England. They’re built on modern cloud infrastructure, API-driven architectures, and shared platform services, and they underpin how genomic data is processed, accessed, and used at national scale

This is not just a feature delivery role; you will use your expertise and your experience to own complex, live systems, enabling the right technical decisions for the products, and shaping platforms that other teams rely on every day.

If you naturally think in systems, properly understand how platform decisions scale impact, and are comfortable operating with a high degree of ownership and ambiguity, this will likely resonate

What you’ll be doing:

  • Own a set of shared data and platform products, responsible for their direction, performance, and long-term evolution.
  • Define clear product strategy and outcomes - align to organisational priorities, set and own squad OKRs, and translate these into focused roadmaps and backlogs.
  • Work closely with engineers and technical leads to shape scalable, API-first architectures that maximise reuse, reduce complexity, and enable teams to move faster.
  • Make decisions about how your products evolve - balancing enhancements, scale, and stability alongside migration away from legacy components where appropriate.
  • Managing products that are part of a regulated environment to meet compliance, audit, and assurance requirements.
  • Use data and well-thought-through proxy metrics to measure impact, particularly where value is indirect or spans multiple teams.
  • Collaborate across squads to shape shared capabilities, reduce duplication, and put in place platform guardrails that enable teams rather than constrain them.
  • Manage stakeholders with clarity and confidence, being transparent on priorities, trade-offs, and risk, and stepping in to resolve issues quickly and decisively.
  • Mentor and support other product managers and team members, fostering a culture of ownership, learning, and continuous improvement.
  • Drive cross-team initiatives that improve platform health, developer experience, and operational efficiency.

What we’re looking for:

  • You are a Senior Product Manager who is experienced and comfortable owning data-focused shared / platform products on which teams across the organisation are dependent.
  • You understand how to manage technical platforms and shared systems as products, with a clear strategy and roadmap, a focus on user needs, and delivering measurable outcomes.
  • You have confidence and experience in managing existing live systems and products (including managing them through the product lifecycle).
  • You are comfortable working directly with engineers to understand and shape longer-term platform evolution.

Qualifications

While your qualifications are certainly important and provide a foundation, we are particularly interested in understanding the breadth and depth of your overall experience, as this gives us a clearer picture of what you can bring to the role.

Additional Information

Salary From: £86,100

Please Note: This is a 1 Year FTC (Fixed Term Contract)

Closing Date: Friday 10th April

Being an integral part of such a meaningful mission is extremely rewarding in itself, but in order to support our people, we’re continually improving our benefits package. We pride ourselves on investing in our people and supporting them to achieve their career goals, as well as offering a benefits package including:

  • Generous Leave: 30 days’ holiday plus bank holidays, additional leave for long service, and the option to apply for up to 30 days of remote working abroad annually (approval required).
  • Family-Friendly: Blended working arrangements, flexible working, enhanced maternity, paternity and shared parental leave benefits.
  • Pension & Financial: Defined contribution pension (Genomics England double-matches up to 10%, however you can contribute more if you wish), Life Assurance (3x salary), and a Give As You Earn scheme.
  • Learning & Development: Individual learning budgets, support for training and certifications, and reimbursement for one annual professional subscription (approval required).
  • Recognition & Rewards: Employee recognition programme and referral scheme.
  • Health & Wellbeing: Subsidised gym membership, a free Headspace account, and access to an Employee Assistance Programme, eye tests, flu jabs.

Equal opportunities and our commitment to a diverse and inclusive workplace

Genomics England is actively committed to providing and supporting an inclusive environment that promotes equity, diversity and inclusion best practice both within our community and in any other area where we have influence. We are proud of our diverse community where everyone can come to work and feel welcomed and treated with respect regardless of any disability, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, religion, sexual orientation, or social background.

Genomics England’s policies of non-discrimination and equity and will be applied fairly to all people, regardless of age, disability, gender identity or reassignment, marital or civil partnership status, being pregnant or recently becoming a parent, race, religion or beliefs, sex or sexual orientation, length of service, whether full or part-time or employed under a permanent or a fixed-term contract or any other relevant factor.

Genomics England does not tolerate any form of discrimination, harassment, victimisation or bullying at work. Such behaviour undermines our mission and core values and diminishes the dignity, respect and integrity of all parties. Our People policies outline our commitment to inclusivity.

We aim to remove barriers in our recruitment processes and to be flexible with our interview processes. Should you require any adjustments that may help you to fully participate in the recruitment process, we encourage you to discuss this with us.

Culture

We have four key behaviours that represent what we would like Genomics England to feel like and the culture we want to encourage, in order for us to achieve our mission. These behaviours help us all work well together, deliver on our outcomes, celebrate our successes and share feedback with each other. You can read about these and other aspects of our culture here Culture | Genomics England

Blended working model

Genomics England operates a blended working model as we know our people appreciate the flexibility that hybrid working can bring. We expect most people to come into the office a minimum of 2 times each month. However, this will vary according to role and will be agreed with your team leader. There is no expectation that people will return to the office full time unless they want to, however, some of our roles require full time on site attendance e.g., lab teams, reception team.

Our teams and squads have, and will continue to reflect on what works best for them to work together successfully and have the freedom to design working patterns to suit, beyond the minimum. Our office locations are: Canary Wharf, Cambridge and Leeds.

Onboarding background checks

As part of our recruitment process, all successful candidates are subject to a Standard Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check.  We therefore require applicants to disclose any previous offences at point of application, as some unspent convictions may mean we are unable to proceed with your application due to the nature of our work in healthcare.

eliminated_jobs.title: Product Manager - Scheduler
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Who we are

80% of the workers across the globe are Deskless. These are the people who keep our lights on and gas flowing, build roads and bridges, run our manufacturing factories, ensure that we get healthcare service, and provide us with reliable phone and internet connectivity. As entrepreneurs, have we considered solving their problems and making them more productive?

Zinier is a company on a mission to empower frontline workers - and the people supporting them - to achieve greater things for themselves and the world around them. With the majority of workers worldwide being deskless, Zinier recognizes the need for Technology Equity to improve the lives and productivity of these workers who keep the world up and running.

We are a remote-first, global team headquartered in Silicon Valley, with a hybrid workforce across the United States, Canada, Europe, Latin America, Singapore, and Bangalore, India, with leading investors that include Accel, ICONIQ Capital, Founders Fund, Newfund Capital, NGP Capital, Tiger Global Management, and Qualcomm Ventures LLC.

What we are looking for

Do you get fired up about the puzzle of putting the right technician on the right job at the right time—and then figuring out how to do it ten times better? Do you instinctively think about scheduling constraints, workforce optimisation, and the gap between what dispatchers need and what technology delivers today? Zinier is searching for a strategically-minded, market-savvy Product Manager to own our Scheduler stream—the most technically demanding and commercially critical pillar of our platform.

In this high-impact role, you’ll own the Scheduler roadmap end-to-end: the ‘what’ and the ‘why’. You’ll spend serious time with customers—sitting with dispatchers, planners, and service delivery leaders—to deeply understand their scheduling requirements, uncover the problems they’re trying to solve, and learn what more they want from their scheduling tools. You’ll pair that customer intimacy with sharp market intelligence: understanding where we differentiate versus competitors, what capabilities are table stakes in the FSM scheduling space, and where the genuine whitespace opportunities lie. That combination is what shapes a roadmap that doesn’t just respond to requests—it positions Zinier’s Scheduler as the category leader.

Scheduling doesn’t exist in isolation. Our Scheduler is deeply interlinked with Dispatch Console, Capacity Management, and Maps & Routes. You’ll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the Product Managers who own those domains and with our Product Architect to ensure coherence across the platform. You’ll also partner with Go-to-Market teams to define the customer journeys and value propositions we want to demonstrate and showcase—it’s not enough to build great scheduling capability; Sales, CS, and Marketing need to articulate why it matters and show it in action.

You won’t be writing user stories or attending daily standups—that’s the Product Owner’s domain. Your PO sits in India, embedded with engineering, and you’ll hand off fully-formed PRDs that give them everything they need to decompose and execute with precision. The cleaner your handoff, the better the output.

Bring your scheduling brain, your customer empathy, your competitive instinct, and your first-principles thinking. This is your opportunity to shape the future of intelligent workforce scheduling—from market positioning to product delivery.

Where you are located

Anywhere in the US; able to collaborate with customers and teams across continents as needed, either remotely or in-person (occasional travel required). You’ll work closely with fellow PMs in the UK/US, Product Architects, and a Product Owner in India, so flexibility across time zones is essential.

What the role offers

  • Own the Scheduler stream roadmap: Define and maintain the product direction for scheduling, dispatching optimisation, capacity management integration, and route optimisation—aligned to portfolio priorities and informed by deep customer and market insight
  • Spend meaningful time with customers: Conduct regular discovery sessions, on-site visits, and workshops with dispatchers, planners, and operations leaders to understand their scheduling requirements, uncover unmet needs, and validate product hypotheses with real-world evidence
  • Build and maintain market intelligence: Continuously assess the competitive landscape—understand where Zinier differentiates, what capabilities are table stakes, and where whitespace opportunities exist—to inform roadmap prioritisation and positioning
  • Partner with Go-to-Market teams: Work with Sales, CS, and Marketing to define the customer journeys and value propositions for Scheduler capabilities; ensure demo environments, sales collateral, and enablement materials showcase differentiated value
  • Collaborate with adjacent Product Managers: Work closely with the PMs who own Dispatch Console, Capacity Management, and Maps & Routes to align priorities, resolve cross-stream dependencies, and ensure the Scheduler roadmap reinforces the broader platform vision
  • Partner with Product Architecture: Engage the Product Architect on technical feasibility, cross-stream dependency mapping, and platform coherence before PRDs enter the backlog
  • Write PRDs with clear problem statements, success criteria, and WSJF scores: Every epic gets a PRD rooted in customer evidence and market context—articulating the problem, who it affects, measurable outcomes, and a rigorous WSJF score
  • Hand off to your Product Owner with precision: Deliver finalised PRDs with wireframes, technical constraints, and clear acceptance criteria that enable the India-based PO to decompose into stories within 3 days
  • Define quarterly OKRs for the Scheduler stream: Set ambitious, measurable objectives aligned to the portfolio priority matrix and track progress monthly
  • Champion evidence-based prioritisation: Synthesise customer insight, NPS feedback, support ticket patterns, usage data, and competitive intelligence into a prioritisation rationale that stakeholders can trus t

What you’ll bring to the role

  • 5–7 years in product management within B2B SaaS: Proven track record owning a product stream, writing PRDs, and delivering measurable outcomes in complex, multi-stakeholder environments
  • Deep domain expertise in scheduling, optimisation, or workforce management: Hands-on experience with scheduling engines, dispatch optimisation, constraint-based assignment, capacity planning, or route optimisation—ideally within field service management, logistics, or workforce management platforms
  • Strong market and competitive instinct: Experience building competitive intelligence, identifying differentiation opportunities, and translating market insight into roadmap decisions—you know the difference between table stakes and genuine advantage
  • Customer discovery expertise: Experienced at running discovery sessions, on-site visits, and workshops with operational users and executives; you know how to extract actionable insight from conversation and observation
  • Exceptional problem framing: You define problems before jumping to solutions; your PRDs start with ‘why’ and ‘for whom’, not ‘build this feature’
  • Strategic thinker with bias to action: Balance long-term vision with tactical execution; make decisions with 70% data, iterate fast, and course-correct without hesitation
  • Cross-functional collaboration: Comfortable working across product streams, aligning with fellow PMs on shared dependencies, and partnering with GTM teams to shape positioning and enablement
  • Stakeholder orchestration skills: Navigate multi-layered organisations, influence without authority, and craft compelling narratives for audiences ranging from field technicians to C-suite executives
  • Data-driven prioritisation: Comfortable with WSJF or equivalent frameworks; you score trade-offs rigorously and can defend sequencing decisions with evidence, not gut feel
  • Technical fluency: You don’t write code, but you understand APIs, data models, and algorithm trade-offs well enough to have credible conversations with engineers and architects
  • Be Hungry. Be Humble. Be Honest. And Hustle.

Own the problem. Know the market. Be the reason our Scheduler becomes the industry standard.

#LI-Remote

eliminated_jobs.title: Project Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Passion for Life, Inc. is a 501©(3) organization. Our mission is to provide forward-thinking career coaching and immersion opportunities driven by the student’s passions and talents in a fun, structured, and high-energy environment, helping under-resourced youth transform their passions into rewarding careers.

Job Description

Passion for Life is seeking a volunteer Project Manager. This is a great role to obtain PDUs or project management experience.  The Project Manager will support the team leaders and team in overseeing and managing projects and initiatives. This role develops, coordinates, and implements project tasks and activities.

Role Responsibilities:

  1. Creating Projects in Asana

  2. Assists the team leader and teams in creating projects and subtasks (steps to achieve the project)

  3. Managing and Communicating Within Projects

  4. Assists team leader by coordinating project tasks to ensure all project milestones are achieved. Frequently follows up with team members to check progress on tasks being performed by the project teams especially those that are overdue or unassigned.

  5. Communicates project activities to team members and PFL’s organization project manager (Director of Project Management).

  6. Create and maintain team meeting minutes and manage the meetings’ project progress and status reports segment. Disseminate meeting recap after each team meeting, assembling any required documentation for projects in Asana.

  7. Report Project Status

  8. Updates project status on Asana and sends updates stakeholders.

  9. Complete and maintain the monthly progress report to share in company meetings.

Qualifications

Desired Skills:

  • Leadership skills

  • Ability to prioritize and manage work against critical project timelines

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

  • Ability to juggle multiple priorities

  • Proven experience in identifying, analyzing, problem-solving and responding to internal and external opportunities and challenges with the ability to translate these into practical plans of action

  • Ability to lead and work collaboratively and support others in the development and implementation of agreed strategies within team and wider organizational contexts

  • Must be a self-starter and possess the ability to work well with others

  • Collaborate on projects as both a team leader and a team member

  • Must possess initiative and creativity; a highly developed work ethic

Additional Information

Qualifications

  • One year of project coordination or management experience (required)

Time Commitment: 8 hours weekly for 6 months

Note: Please apply if your current location is USA (Central and EST), other locations are not eligible

eliminated_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager - Security
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
About The Team

This role is within our Security organization, which supports Cloud Security, Product Security, Enterprise Security, and Detection & Response. This organization partners with Product, Engineering, Privacy, GRC, IT, and Legal teams to ensure security best practices are applied to protect the company and Outreach customers.

The Role

Are you passionate about contributing to the success of a cybersecurity program through program management? We are seeking an experienced Technical Program Manager to join our Security

Team. In this role you will drive security programs and execute security projects that will assist executing a strategy to improve security posture. You will provide operational support for cross-functional teams, such as engineering, product, privacy, and legal. You will translate complex technical, regulatory, and security requirements into structured programs, clear technical requirements, and measurable outcomes.

Location: We are open to remote.

Your Daily Adventures Will Include:

  • Apply your technical understanding of Cybersecurity in a hand-on role, driving security programs and providing TPM support

  • Coordinate and track services provided by the security team and assist with enforcement security requirements throughout the organization

  • Assist with prioritizing work resulting from security findings, stakeholder requests, and strategic vision

  • Work with capability owners and stakeholders to develop a roadmap, capacity planning, and delivery planning for the team

  • Represent the Security organization on customer calls and talk about the Outreach platform and security processes

  • Identify, assess, and manage security risks across projects and systems

  • Manage dependencies across cross-functional teams and resolve blockers

  • Track and report on security program status, metrics, and key performance indicators (KPIs) to stakeholders and leadership

  • Manage demand intake, triage, and prioritization of Security service requests

  • Define and manage dependencies on external teams to improve security posture by getting alignment on commitments

  • Partner with Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) team to provide evidence to auditors that demonstrate efficacy of security controls

Our Vision of You:

  • 5+ years of Cybersecurity experience in a Technical Program Manager in a fast-paced, SaaS software company.

  • Understanding of cybersecurity principles, including Network Security, Cloud Security (AWS, Azure, or GCP), Application Security, Identity and Access Management (IAM)

  • Experienced with service management and process engineering

  • Successful at using a data-driven approach to track service performance and influence decision making

  • Experience managing cross-functional, large-scale technical security programs, including the Security Vulnerability Program, Security Exceptions Program, and Bug Bounty Program

  • Familiar with security tooling and system integrations

  • Experience leading an External Penetration Test end-to-end, by managing the vendor, defining and prioritizing the work, writing customer-facing reports, and having technical conversations with cross-functional teams to remediate the findings

  • Familiarity with security frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2)

  • Excellent communication, organizational, and leadership skills

$130,000 - $170,000 a year

The annual on target earnings (OTE) range for this role is $130,000- $170,000. You may also be offered incentive compensation, bonus, restricted stock units, and benefits. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate’s skills, qualifications, location and experience. Final offers are determined through a holistic assessment and will vary within the posted range. Your Recruiter will share specific details based on your location and role during the hiring process.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

eliminated_jobs.title: Senior AI Product Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
At Zego, we know that traditional motor insurance holds good drivers back. It’s too complicated, too expensive, and it doesn’t take into account how well you actually drive.

That’s why, since 2016, we’ve been on a mission to change all of that. Our mission at Zego is to offer the lowest priced insurance for good drivers.

From van drivers and gig workers to everyday car drivers, our customers are our driving force — they’re at the heart of everything we do.

We’ve sold tens of millions of policies so far, and raised over $200 million in funding. And we’re only just getting started.

About the role

We are looking for a Senior AI Product Manager who doesn’t just talk about AI. They build with it, ship with it, and use it to fundamentally change how products are made.

This is not a role for someone who wants to run a tidy backlog and attend stand-ups. This is a role for a builder. Someone who has stared at a blank page and turned it into something real. Someone who moves at a pace that makes others uncomfortable, makes bold calls with imperfect information, and holds themselves accountable to outcomes, not outputs.

You will work directly with Zego’s leadership team, operating at the intersection of product, data, and engineering. This is a rare opportunity to define the AI product playbook at a high-growth insurtech, from scratch.

Why this role, why now

AI is not a feature at Zego, it’s a strategic priority. We’re at an inflection point where the decisions made in the next 12 months will define how we serve customers, how we operate as a business, and how fast we can grow. This role sits right at the centre of that.

If you want a role where you can point at something real and say “I built that”, this is it.

What you will be doing

  • Build 0-to-1 AI products. Lead the discovery, design, and delivery of new AI-powered features and propositions, from loose hypothesis to live product. You’re not handing this off; you’re in the trenches.
  • Move fast and validate faster. Design lightweight experiments to test AI hypotheses before committing engineering resource. You know that speed of learning is the leading metric, and you architect your work accordingly.
  • Embed AI across the product organisation. Identify where AI tooling (LLMs, agents, automation) can materially accelerate how our PMs, designers, and engineers work. Then make it happen, not in a deck, but in practice.
  • Translate complexity into clarity. Work closely with ML engineers, data scientists, and software engineers to understand what’s technically feasible and push the boundaries of it. You can engage credibly on model behaviour, data pipelines, and system design.
  • Define and track what matters. Set rigorous success metrics for every AI initiative. You’re comfortable building the measurement framework from scratch and iterating on it as you learn.
  • Be a force multiplier for the team. Mentor and upskill other PMs on AI-native ways of working. Help build a culture where AI is a default tool, not an afterthought.

What you will need to be successful

  • You have a founder’s mindset. You don’t wait for permission, resources, or perfect conditions. You find a way. You own outcomes end-to-end and hold yourself to a high bar
  • You’ve built things from scratch. You have concrete examples of 0-to-1 product work, ideally with an AI/ML component. You know what it takes to go from nothing to something people actually use.
  • You’re an AI practitioner, not just an AI enthusiast. You use AI tools daily. You’ve shipped AI-powered features. You understand the difference between a fine-tuned model and a RAG pipeline, and you know when each is the right call.
  • You move at pace, with rigour. Fast doesn’t mean sloppy. You design clean experiments, make data-grounded decisions, and build things that scale. You just do it faster than most.
  • You’re commercially sharp. You connect AI capability to business value instinctively. You think in terms of unit economics, conversion, retention, and ROI, not just model accuracy or technical elegance.
  • You can go deep technically. You’re not an engineer, but you’re not intimidated by engineers either. You can read a system diagram, interrogate a data pipeline, and challenge architectural decisions with confidence.
  • You communicate with clarity and conviction. Whether you’re briefing the leadership team, aligning a cross-functional squad, or writing a one-pager at 11pm, you make complex ideas land simply and persuasively.
  • You’re genuinely curious and relentlessly resourceful. The AI landscape changes weekly. You stay ahead of it, experiment constantly, and bring new ideas to the table before anyone asks.

What’s it like to work at Zego?

Joining Zego is a career-defining move. People go further here, reaching their full potential to achieve extraordinary things.

We’re spread throughout the UK and Europe, and united by our drive to get things done. We’re proud of our company and our culture – a friendly and inclusive space where we can lift each other up and celebrate our wins every day.

Together, we’re setting the bar higher, delivering exceptional work that makes a difference. Our people are the most important part of our story, and everyone here plays a role. There’s loads of room to learn and grow, and you’ll get the freedom to steer your career wherever you want.

You’ll work alongside a talented group who embrace each other’s differences and aren’t afraid of a challenge. We recognise our achievements, learn from our mistakes, and help each other to be the best we can be. Together, we’re making insurance matter.

How we work

We believe that teams work better when they have time to collaborate and space to get things done. We call it Zego Hybrid. We ask you to spend at least one day a week in our central London office. We think it’s a good mix of collaborative face time and flexible home-working, setting us up to achieve the right balance between work and life.

Our approach to AI

We believe in the power of AI to meaningfully improve how we work - helping us move faster, think differently, and focus on what matters most. At Zego, we encourage people to stay curious and intentional about how AI is leveraged in their work and teams to drive practical impact every day. This is your chance to do the most meaningful work of your career - and we’ll provide you with the tools, support, and freedom to do it well.

Benefits

We reward our people well. Join us and you’ll get a market-competitive salary, private medical insurance, company share options, generous holiday allowance, and a whole lot of wellbeing benefits. We also offer an annual flexible hybrid working contribution, which you can use to support with your travel to the office or towards your own personal development. And that’s just for starters!

There’s more to Zego than just a job - Check out our blog for insights, stories, and more.

We’re an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability status.

#LI-IL1

#LI-Hybrid

eliminated_jobs.title: Clinical Program Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
About us:

Foodsmart is the leading telenutrition and foodcare solution, backed by a robust network of Registered Dietitians. Our platform is designed to foster healthier food choices, drive lasting behavior change, and deliver long-term health outcomes. Through our highly personalized, digital platform, we guide our 2.2 million members—including those in employer-sponsored health plans, regional and national Medicaid managed care organizations, Medicare Advantage plans, and commercial insurers—on a tailored journey to eating well while saving time and money.

Foodsmart seamlessly integrates dietary assessments and nutrition counseling with online food ordering and cost-effective meal planning for the entire family, optimizing ingredients both at home and on the go. We partner with national and regional retailers across the U.S., many of whom accept SNAP/EBT, making healthier food more accessible. Additionally, we assist members with SNAP enrollment and management, providing tangible access to nutritious food. In 2024, Foodsmart secured a $200 million investment from TPG’s Rise Fund, which supports entrepreneurs dedicated to achieving the United Nations’ Sustainable Development Goals. This investment will help us expand our reach, particularly to low-income workers who are disproportionately affected by diet-related diseases.

At Foodsmart, our mission is to make nutritious food accessible and affordable for everyone, regardless of economic status. We are committed to a set of core values that shape our culture and work environment:

👥 Customer First - You start with the member and work backwards.

🚀 Make It Happen - You act with urgency, use data, and hold high standards.

🤝 One Team - You collaborate with respect and commit as a group.

Whether you’re a dietitian, a commercial leader, or a technologist, working at Foodsmart means being part of a team that is passionate, supportive, and driven by a shared purpose. Join us in transforming the way people access and enjoy healthy food.

About the role:

The Clinical Program Manager is the central “quarterback” for our new and existing client onboarding and clinical programs to ensure they are effectively implemented and aligned with organizational goals. You will own the end-to-end lifecycle of new client launches and ensure the ongoing success of existing clinical programs. This role sits at the intersection of Client Success, Sales, Credentialing, Training and Clinical Ops, requiring a high-stakes collaborator who can turn ambiguity into organized project plans. The ideal candidate is a detail-obsessed project manager who can hold cross-functional teams accountable to deadlines while using data to make projections, and track and improve program performance.

You will:

  • Client Launch Ownership: Lead the implementation of new client launches, mapping out clinical workflows and serving as the primary bridge between clients, dietitians, and clinical operations to ensure seamless onboarding.
  • Client Success Management: Provide ongoing oversight of client success by monitoring clinical KPIs and supply and demand needs.
  • Cross-Functional Coordination: Act as the central point of contact for Clinical Ops, Client Success, Training, Sales, and Credentialing, ensuring all deliverables and information are synced across teams.
  • Project Management & Accountability: Develop and maintain high-detail project plans (timelines, milestones, resource allocation). Proactively identify risks and hold team members accountable to critical deadlines.
  • Data Analysis: Use Excel/Google Sheets to perform intermediate data analysis on program performance, outcomes, and operational efficiency to drive evidence-based adjustments.
  • Network Support: Collaborate with our Registered Dietitian (RD) network to facilitate training, troubleshoot implementation hurdles, and integrate provider feedback into program enhancements.
  • Regulatory Alignment: Coordinate with our Credentialing team to ensure all programs align with healthcare regulations, with a specific focus on the unique requirements of Medicare and Medicaid populations, at both state and national levels.

You have:

Qualifications

  • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Healthcare Administration, Business, or a related field preferred

  • Experience: 3+ years of project management or operations experience in a healthcare setting.

  • Technical Savvy: Intermediate to advanced proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets (VLOOKUPs, Pivot Tables, data visualization) and experience with PM tools (Asana, ClickUp, Salesforce or similar preferred).

  • The “Quarterback” Mindset: Proven ability to lead through influence, navigate ambiguity, and make decisions with minimal direction.

  • Communication: Exceptional relationship-building skills; able to translate complex operational needs into clear action items for different stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications

  • Familiarity with Medicare and Medicaid health plans and populations

  • Experience with Quality Improvement (Lean, Six Sigma) or EHR systems

  • Clinical experience as a dietitian and/or clinical workflow mapping

  • PMP or equivalent

  • Project Management certification

$70,000 - $80,000 a year

Role: Clinical Program Manager

Level: Manager

Location: Remote

Base Salary Range: $70,000-80,000 + performance-based bonus

About our benefits and perks:

Remote-First Company

Flexible Unlimited PTO

Healthcare Coverage (Medical, Dental, Vision)

401k and FSA

Foodsmart is an Equal Opportunity Employer. It is our firm policy to extend equal employment and advancement opportunity to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship status, religious creed, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, medical conditions related to pregnancy, childbirth and/or breastfeeding), gender, gender identity and expression, sexual orientation, marital status, disability (physical or mental) and/or a medical condition, genetic information, ancestry, veteran status or service in the uniformed services, and any other characteristic protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

eliminated_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager - Security
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
About The Team

This role is within our Security organization, which supports Cloud Security, Product Security, Enterprise Security, and Detection & Response. This organization partners with Product, Engineering, Privacy, GRC, IT, and Legal teams to ensure security best practices are applied to protect the company and Outreach customers.

The Role

Are you passionate about contributing to the success of a cybersecurity program through program management? We are seeking an experienced Technical Program Manager to join our Security

Team. In this role you will drive security programs and execute security projects that will assist executing a strategy to improve security posture. You will provide operational support for cross-functional teams, such as engineering, product, privacy, and legal. You will translate complex technical, regulatory, and security requirements into structured programs, clear technical requirements, and measurable outcomes.

Location: We are open to remote.

Your Daily Adventures Will Include:

  • Apply your technical understanding of Cybersecurity in a hand-on role, driving security programs and providing TPM support

  • Coordinate and track services provided by the security team and assist with enforcement security requirements throughout the organization

  • Assist with prioritizing work resulting from security findings, stakeholder requests, and strategic vision

  • Work with capability owners and stakeholders to develop a roadmap, capacity planning, and delivery planning for the team

  • Represent the Security organization on customer calls and talk about the Outreach platform and security processes

  • Identify, assess, and manage security risks across projects and systems

  • Manage dependencies across cross-functional teams and resolve blockers

  • Track and report on security program status, metrics, and key performance indicators (KPIs) to stakeholders and leadership

  • Manage demand intake, triage, and prioritization of Security service requests

  • Define and manage dependencies on external teams to improve security posture by getting alignment on commitments

  • Partner with Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) team to provide evidence to auditors that demonstrate efficacy of security controls

Our Vision of You:

  • 5+ years of Cybersecurity experience in a Technical Program Manager in a fast-paced, SaaS software company.

  • Understanding of cybersecurity principles, including Network Security, Cloud Security (AWS, Azure, or GCP), Application Security, Identity and Access Management (IAM)

  • Experienced with service management and process engineering

  • Successful at using a data-driven approach to track service performance and influence decision making

  • Experience managing cross-functional, large-scale technical security programs, including the Security Vulnerability Program, Security Exceptions Program, and Bug Bounty Program

  • Familiar with security tooling and system integrations

  • Experience leading an External Penetration Test end-to-end, by managing the vendor, defining and prioritizing the work, writing customer-facing reports, and having technical conversations with cross-functional teams to remediate the findings

  • Familiarity with security frameworks (e.g., NIST, ISO 27001, SOC 2)

  • Excellent communication, organizational, and leadership skills

$130,000 - $170,000 a year

The annual on target earnings (OTE) range for this role is $130,000- $170,000. You may also be offered incentive compensation, bonus, restricted stock units, and benefits. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate’s skills, qualifications, location and experience. Final offers are determined through a holistic assessment and will vary within the posted range. Your Recruiter will share specific details based on your location and role during the hiring process.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

eliminated_jobs.title: Project Manager, Electrical
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Turner & Townsend is a global professional services company with over 22,000 people in more than 60 countries.

Working with our clients across real estate, infrastructure, energy and natural resources, we transform together delivering outcomes that improve people’s lives. Working in partnership makes it possible to deliver the world’s most impactful projects and programmes as we turn challenge into opportunity and complexity into success.

Our capabilities include programme, project, cost, asset and commercial management, controls and performance, procurement and supply chain, net zero and digital solutions.

We are majority-owned by CBRE Group, Inc., the world’s largest commercial real estate services and investment firm, with our partners holding a significant minority interest. Turner & Townsend and CBRE work together to provide clients with the premier programme, project and cost management offering in markets around the world

Who are you?

You are an experienced Project Manager, Electrical with a minimum of 10 years post-graduate experience looking for the next step or are currently undertaking a similar role in a similar environment

Job Description

Project Development

  • Define project objectives, deliverables, and success criteria in collaboration with operations, engineering, and maintenance stakeholders.
  • Develop the project scope of work, including technical requirements
  • Identify project constraints, risks, and constructability considerations.
  • Develop class 2 cost estimates, schedules, and execution strategies.
  • Prepare documentation required to advance projects through the BHP toll gate process.
  • Support procurement and contract award by creating RFP scopes of supply, reviewing vendor proposals and aligning with budget allowances.

Design Coordination

  • Coordinate engineering disciplines supporting underground conveyor design, including:
    • Mechanical systems
    • Structural supports
    • Electrical and controls
    • Civil / underground infrastructure
    • Facilitate design reviews and ensure stakeholder alignment.
    • Ensure designs meet operational, safety, and maintainability requirements.
  • Manage and finalize partially completed engineering deliverables including:
    • Layouts
    • Equipment specifications
    • Installation methodologies
    • Design packages
    • Identify long-lead materials and procurement requirements.

Procurement & Logistics

  • Inventory existing materials
  • Develop procurement strategies for conveyor systems, structural components, and supporting infrastructure.
  • Coordinate with supply chain teams to:
    • Prepare bid packages
    • Evaluate vendor proposals
    • Award purchase orders
    • Manage procurement of long-lead items
  • Ensure timely delivery of materials aligned with the project schedule.

Execution & Field Management

  • Direct construction, assisted by Project Coordinators
  • Manage contractors on site, ensuring safety, quality, and productivity targets are achieved.
  • Maintain cost control throughout execution, providing accurate forecasts and variance reporting.
  • Facilitate weekly contractor meetings
  • Resolve field issues by engaging with design teams and operations group.
  • Track productivity and unit rates to inform ongoing and future estimates.

Health, Safety, Environment & Compliance

  • Ensure implementation of project specific HSE management systems.
  • Ensure compliance with legislative, organizational, and site standards,
  • Conduct site inspections, attend toolbox talks, and participate in incident investigations.

Project Closeout

  • Deliver completed scopes of supply
  • Compile as-built drawings, blacklines, turnover packages, and lessons learned.
  • Conduct post-project review with operations and engineering teams.

Qualifications

Education & Experience

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering:
    • Electrical Engineering
    • Mechanical Engineering
  • 10+ Years of industry relevant experience
    • Including 7+ years project management experience

OR

  • Engineering Technologist / Technical Diploma
  • 12+ Years of industry relevant experience
    • Including 7+ years project management experience

Technical Skills

  • Commitment to Safety
  • Ability to manage small to medium-sized projects or workstreams.
  • Experience in preparing technical reports, documentation, and presentations.
  • Understanding of budgeting, scheduling, and resource planning.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in cross-functional teams.

Additional Information

What’s in it for you…

  • 100% covered health and dental benefits for you and your dependents (including paramedical services)

  • Health spending account, to top up expenses not covered in the benefits program

  • RRSP option with company matching

  • Annual learning and development budget

  • Access to a range of online learning tools, and support for career development and growth

  • Coverage of one professional membership or license fee per year, if directly related to your role

  • We believe in work-life balance, so you can recharge and take care of what matters most with vacation, flex and sick days

  • A flexible, hybrid work approach that allows colleagues to find balance between their professional and personal lives

  • Commitment to creating a diverse, equitable and inclusive culture that promotes a sense of belonging

This posting reflects an existing vacancy that we are actively hiring for.

Our inspired people share our vision and mission. We provide a great place to work, where each person has the opportunity and voice to affect change. We want our people to succeed both in work and life. To support this we promote a healthy, productive and flexible working environment that respects work-life balance.

Turner & Townsend is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees and actively encourage applications from all sectors of the community.

Turner & Townsend does not accept any speculative or unsolicited CV’s that have been sent to our internal recruitment team or hiring managers from agencies outside of our preferred supplier list. Any speculative or unsolicited CV’s will be treated as a direct application.

It is strictly against Turner & Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner & Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.

We may use automated or AI-based tools to support the screening, assessment, or selection of applicants.

Please find out more about us at www.turnerandtownsend.com/

#LI-SA1

#LI-Hybrid

Join our social media conversations for more information about Turner & Townsend and our exciting future projects:

Twitter

Instagram

LinkedIn

It is strictly against Turner & Townsend policy for candidates to pay any fee in relation to our recruitment process. No recruitment agency working with Turner & Townsend will ask candidates to pay a fee at any time.

Any unsolicited resumes/CVs submitted through our website or to Turner & Townsend personal e-mail accounts, are considered property of Turner & Townsend and are not subject to payment of agency fees. eliminated_jobs.title: Project Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Company Description

Passion for Life, Inc. is a 501©(3) organization. Our mission is to provide forward-thinking career coaching and immersion opportunities driven by the student’s passions and talents in a fun, structured, and high-energy environment, helping under-resourced youth transform their passions into rewarding careers.

Job Description

Passion for Life is seeking a volunteer Project Manager. This is a great role to obtain PDUs or project management experience.  The Project Manager will support the team leaders and team in overseeing and managing projects and initiatives. This role develops, coordinates, and implements project tasks and activities.

Role Responsibilities:

  1. Creating Projects in Asana

  2. Assists the team leader and teams in creating projects and subtasks (steps to achieve the project)

  3. Managing and Communicating Within Projects

  4. Assists team leader by coordinating project tasks to ensure all project milestones are achieved. Frequently follows up with team members to check progress on tasks being performed by the project teams especially those that are overdue or unassigned.

  5. Communicates project activities to team members and PFL’s organization project manager (Director of Project Management).

  6. Create and maintain team meeting minutes and manage the meetings’ project progress and status reports segment. Disseminate meeting recap after each team meeting, assembling any required documentation for projects in Asana.

  7. Report Project Status

  8. Updates project status on Asana and sends updates stakeholders.

  9. Complete and maintain the monthly progress report to share in company meetings.

Qualifications

Desired Skills:

  • Leadership skills

  • Ability to prioritize and manage work against critical project timelines

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

  • Ability to juggle multiple priorities

  • Proven experience in identifying, analyzing, problem-solving and responding to internal and external opportunities and challenges with the ability to translate these into practical plans of action

  • Ability to lead and work collaboratively and support others in the development and implementation of agreed strategies within team and wider organizational contexts

  • Must be a self-starter and possess the ability to work well with others

  • Collaborate on projects as both a team leader and a team member

  • Must possess initiative and creativity; a highly developed work ethic

Additional Information

Qualifications

  • One year of project coordination or management experience (required)

Time Commitment: 8 hours weekly for 6 months

Note: Please apply if your current location is USA (Central and EST), other locations are not eligible

eliminated_jobs.title: Project Manager Tax & Legal
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Voleon is a technology company that applies state-of-the-art AI and machine learning techniques to real-world problems in finance. For nearly two decades, we have led our industry and worked at the frontier of applying AI/ML to investment management. We have become a multibillion-dollar asset manager, and we have ambitious goals for the future.

Your colleagues will include internationally recognized experts in artificial intelligence and machine learning research as well as highly experienced finance and technology professionals.  The people who shape our company come from other backgrounds, including concert music performances, humanitarian aid, opera singing, sports writing, and BMX racing. You will be part of a team that loves to succeed together.

In addition to our enriching and collegial working environment, we offer highly competitive compensation and benefits packages, technology talks by our experts, a beautiful modern office, daily catered lunches, and more.

We are seeking an experienced Project Manager, Tax and Legal to join the Legal team, reporting directly to the General Counsel. In this role, you will lead cross-functional initiatives related to tax planning, compliance, corporate structuring, and regulatory matters. In addition, you will oversee outside tax counsel and other related advisors, ensuring work is scoped appropriately, delivered on time, and aligned with the firm’s objectives.

You will partner with teams across the company to coordinate complex tax and legal projects, including entity structuring, domestic and international tax compliance, and other strategic initiatives. You will work closely with both internal stakeholders and external advisors to drive execution, improve processes, and help scale our legal and tax infrastructure. This role offers the opportunity to shape how we manage significant tax matters and outside counsel relationships, and to build durable systems that support the firm’s long-term growth.

This role is open to remote work in the US or hybrid in our Berkeley or New York office.

Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end delivery of tax and legal projects, defining objectives, timelines, budgets, and success metrics.

  • Develop detailed project plans; establish clear ownership and prioritization; monitor progress and report status to senior stakeholders.

  • Manage outside tax counsel and other advisors, including scoping engagements, tracking deliverables, and monitoring spend against budget.

  • Document and improve legal and tax processes; design operating models for new structures, regulatory requirements, and compliance initiatives; identify opportunities for efficiency and risk reduction.

  • Communicate clearly with senior leadership, providing concise updates, sound recommendations, and managing change to ensure adoption of new processes.

  • Mentor junior team members or analysts supporting legal and tax workstreams.

  • Provide additional support to the broader Legal team on operational or strategic initiatives as needed.

Requirements

  • 5+ years of relevant experience in project management.

  • Exposure to the following is a plus, but not required: tax, legal operations, or related functions, ideally within a hedge fund, asset manager, law firm, or professional services environment.

  • Proven experience managing complex, cross-functional projects in fast-paced environments, including coordination with outside counsel.

  • Bachelor’s degree required; JD, CPA, MBA, or other advanced degree a plus.

  • Authorization to work in the United States.

The base salary range for this position is $150,000 - $175,000 in the location(s) of this posting. Individual salaries are determined through a variety of factors, including, but not limited to, education, experience, knowledge, skills, and geography. Base salary does not include other forms of total compensation such as bonus compensation and other benefits. Our benefits package includes medical, dental and vision coverage, life and AD&D insurance, 20 days of paid time off, 9 sick days, and a 401(k) plan with a company match.

“Friends of Voleon” Candidate Referral Program

If you have a great candidate in mind for this role and would like to have the potential to earn $7,500 if your referred candidate is successfully hired and employed by The Voleon Group, please use this form to submit your referral. For more details regarding eligibility, terms and conditions please make sure to review the Voleon Referral Bonus Program.

Equal Opportunity Employer

The Voleon Group is an Equal Opportunity employer. Applicants are considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, age, sex, gender, marital status, sexual orientation and identity, genetic information, veteran status, citizenship, or any other factors prohibited by local, state, or federal law.

Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

eliminated_jobs.title: Technical Program Manager
eliminated_jobs.jd_full:
Are you an accomplished Technical Program Manager looking to apply your expertise to drive complex, cross-functional initiatives and deliver impactful outcomes? In this role, you will lead and coordinate client project work across multiple practices, including Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Decision Sciences, and Analytics Strategy, ensuring seamless execution and alignment with business objectives.

Aimpoint Digital is a premier analytics consulting firm with a mission to drive business value for clients through expertise in data strategy, data analytics, decision sciences, and data engineering and infrastructure. We’re a dynamic team committed to solving our clients’ most critical business challenges in partnership with the industry’s most innovative cloud and data technology providers. What sets us apart is our approach: we start by truly listening, then craft tailored, scalable solutions powered by modern technologies, delivered by our passionate consulting experts. Joining our team means working alongside some of the brightest minds in data and AI consulting to lead complex programs and solve meaningful problems for our clients.

What you will do

Are you an accomplished senior technical program manager looking to apply your level of expertise to drive meaningful projects and portfolios of work forward. Specifically, help support client project work across multiple practices ranging from Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Decision Sciences and Analytics Strategy.

Aimpoint Digital is a premier analytics consulting firm with a mission to drive business value for clients through expertise in data strategy, data analytics, decision sciences, and data engineering and infrastructure. We’re a dynamic team committed to solving our client’s most critical business challenges in partnership with the industry’s most innovative cloud and data technology providers. What sets us apart is our approach: we start by truly listening, then craft tailored solutions powered by modern technologies, which are delivered by our passionate consulting experts. Joining our team means working alongside some of the brightest minds in data and AI consulting to solve meaningful problems for our clients.

You will:

  • Become a trusted advisor working together with our clients, from data owners and analytic users to C-level executives

  • Drive multi-workstream programs delivering enterprise data and AI platforms and measurable business outcomes

  • Serve as the primary client-facing lead for project planning, delivery, risk management, and communication

  • Own program governance, including consolidated status reporting, issue/risk escalation, and stakeholder engagement

Who we are looking for

We are building a diverse team of talented and motivated people who deeply understand business problems and enjoy solving them, collaborating across disciplines to deliver solutions.

You are a proactive and driven project leader with a proven ability to manage diverse, high-visibility initiatives of varying size and complexity. Someone who is passionate about delivering successful outcomes through structured execution, transparent communication, and strong stakeholder alignment. You thrive on complex or ambiguous challenges, providing the right balance of strategic direction and hands-on support to support technical delivery, strengthen client relationships, and continuously identify opportunities to create additional value. You are a skilled communicator who can help our teams demonstrate and articulate a project’s progress and overall value that Aimpoint brings to a client.

As a Program Manager, you will be expected to be a trusted advisor and advocate for the PMO, overseeing programs comprised of multiple projects, and reinforcing delivery best practices. You will collaborate closely with the various practices within Aimpoint providing ownership and support on both client engagements and internal projects.

Specific technical qualifications as follows:

  • 5+ years leading technical delivery in consulting/professional services, including multi-workstream programs and cross-functional teams.

  • Proven ability to translate technical deliverables into measurable business outcomes and executive-ready communication.

  • Experience delivering modern data, analytics, and/or AI solutions in cloud and enterprise environments; able to partner effectively with engineers, architects, and analytics leaders.

  • Strong program governance and delivery management skills: integrated plans, dependency management, RAID, change control, and clear escalation paths.

  • Fluency across Agile, hybrid, and/or Waterfall delivery; able to adapt the approach to client context and team maturity.

  • Excellent stakeholder management, facilitation, and communication skills, including executive-level reporting.

  • Willingness to travel to client sites as needed.

We are actively seeking candidates for full-time, hybrid work with weekly in-person expectations in London to work with our technology partners, clients and colleagues in our London Office.
